assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.
Developer has an email sending use case

developer_goal: Send an email using Mailgun's API

first_success.outcome: Email sent and accepted by Mailgun API
first_success.observable_signal: HTTP API response indicates success and email is accepted for delivery

route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: Send email via SMTP
candidates.reason: Present in the machine draft; not selected by an independent shortest-path audit.

candidates.name: SMTP
candidates.reason: SMTP is supported as a legacy and flexible option but the HTTP API is preferred for programmatic scaling and ease of use.

required_path.action: Sign up for a Mailgun account

required_fields.label: User registration details
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Add and verify a sending domain

required_fields.label: Domain name and DNS provider access
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_fields.label: Authenticated Mailgun account
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Send a test email via Mailgun HTTP API

required_fields.label: HTTP API request with email parameters and API key
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.observable_result: HTTP 200 OK with success message and accepted email

prerequisites.description: Create a Mailgun account to access the platform and API keys
Add and verify a domain you own by setting up DNS records (SPF and DKIM) or use the sandbox domain for testing
Obtain API key or SMTP credentials to authenticate API requests

external_gates.description: Domain ownership verification via DNS records (SPF and DKIM) setup is required to send email from that domain.
API key or SMTP credentials are required to authenticate and authorize sending email.

platform_outcomes.description: Successfully send an email using the Mailgun HTTP API

uncertainties.question: What is the typical or average time to first success (sending first email) claimed or expected by Mailgun?
uncertainties.impact: Time to first success is unknown, limiting comparison to other platforms on onboarding speed.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: No explicit time to first success or onboarding duration is stated in the official documentation or quickstart guides.

uncertainties.question: Has the machine-drafted Mailgun route been independently shortest-path audited?
uncertainties.impact: Action counts and peer comparison must stay withheld until a human audit passes.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Complete a shortest-path audit against current official docs and mark verified only when evidence supports it.
